page: UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-16GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 shared vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, alongside 16.0 GB of system memory, which translates to 4.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 50 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top tier. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores, fall into the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register average performance, while the PassMark memory score is positioned in the bottom tier, indicating a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive operations.

Given its shared CPU allocation and starter-tier classification, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require high single-threaded performance but do not continuously saturate all cores. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, basic database operations, and lightweight background processing. It is less suitable for memory-heavy applications or medium-to-large language model inference due to weaker memory performance and lower multi-threaded scaling.
description: The UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 4 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor at 2.0 GHz. It features 50 GB of local SSD storage and includes one public IPv4 address. Architecturally, the server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though its multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth align with average industry benchmarks. Its memory subsystem shows weaker performance in PassMark testing. This resource profile makes the server a cost-effective option for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and lightweight database operations that benefit from strong single-threaded execution without requiring dedicated CPU resources or massive memory throughput.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-4xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 50 GB SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core speed for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with balanced memory and local SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
